title: Photodynamic insecticides
abstract: Disclosed herein are photodynamic insecticide methods and compositions for the control or reduction of insect populations comprising the use of photosensitizer compounds in combination with light.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for reducing an insect population, the method comprising: (a) contacting the insect population with a photosensitizer, wherein the photosensitizer is internalized by larval and/or adult insects within the insect population, wherein the photosensitizer is a phthalocyanine or porphyrin derivative compound, and wherein the photosensitizer produces reactive oxygen species upon excitation by light; and (b) exposing the photosensitized insect population to light in the presence of oxygen to reduce the insect population. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the photosensitizer is a compound of formula (I): or an acceptable salt thereof, wherein M is Zn or Si(L 1 )(L 2 ); L 1 and L 2 are independently selected fromГўВҖВ“O(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“O(C 1 -C 6 alkenyl), ГўВҲВ’O(C 1 -C 6 alkynyl),ГўВҖВ“NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 , andГўВҖВ“OR, wherein each R is independentlyГўВҖВ“[C 1 -C 6 alkylene-O] m -R',ГўВҖВ“[C 1 -C 6 alkylene- NR"] n -R', orГўВҲВ’Si(RГўВҖВҙ) 3 , each m and n are independently an integer selected from 1 to 20, each R' is independently selected from H and C 1 -C 6 alkyl, each R" is independently selected from H and C 1 -C 6 alkyl, each RГўВҖВҙ is independently selected from H, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, and aryl; each q is independently an integer selected from 0, 1, and 2; and R 1 , R 2 , R 3 , and R 4 are independently selected from halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, C 1 - C 6 alkenyl, C 1 -C 6 alkynyl,ГўВҖВ“NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 , C 1 -C 6 alkoxy, C 1 -C 6 aryloxy, C 1 -C 6 heteroaryloxy, and polyalkylene oxide, 

 each optionally substituted with one or more of halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl,ГўВҖВ“ OH, C 1 -C 6 alkoxy,ГўВҖВ“NH 2 ,ГўВҖВ“NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 ,ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (I),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (Cl), orГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (F); or the photosensitizer is a compound of formula (II): or an acceptable salt thereof, wherein each p is independently an integer selected from 0, 1, and 2; and R 11 , R 12 , R 13 , and R 14 are independently selected from the group consisiting of: halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, C 1 -C 6 alkenyl, C 1 -C 6 alkynyl, C 1 -C 6 alkoxy,ГўВҖВ“ NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 ,ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (I),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (Cl),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (F),ГўВҲВ’S(O) 2 (OH), andГўВҲВ’S(O) 2 (C 1 -C 6 alkoxy). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the photosensitizer is selected from the group consisting of: PC1, PC2, PC3.4, PC14, TMAP, and TPPS2. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the photosensitizer is selected from the group consisting of PC1, PC2, PC3. 4, and PC14. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the light is white light or red light. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the insect population comprises Anopheles spp., Aedes spp., Culex spp., Cocquilletidia spp., Phlebotomus spp., Simulium spp., or Culicoides spp. insects. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the insect population comprises hematophagus insects. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the photosensitizer is internalized by 2 nd , 3 rd , and/or 4 th instar larval insects. 9. An insecticidal composition comprising a photosensitizer selected from the group consisting of: a compound of formula (I) or a compound of formula (II), wherein the compound of formula (I) is: or an acceptable salt thereof, wherein M is Zn or Si(L 1 )(L 2 ); L 1 and L 2 are independently selected fromГўВҖВ“O(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“O(C 1 -C 6 alkenyl), ГўВҲВ’O(C 1 -C 6 alkynyl),ГўВҖВ“NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 , andГўВҖВ“OR, wherein each R is independentlyГўВҖВ“[C 1 -C 6 alkylene-O] m -R',ГўВҖВ“[C 1 -C 6 alkylene- NR"] n -R', orГўВҲВ’Si(RГўВҖВҙ) 3 , each m and n are independently an integer selected from 1 to 20, each R' is independently selected from H and C 1 -C 6 alkyl, each R" is independently selected from H and C 1 -C 6 alkyl, each RГўВҖВҙ is independently selected from H, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, and aryl; each q is independently an integer selected from 0, 1, and 2; and R 1 , R 2 , R 3 , and R 4 are independently selected from halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, C 1 - C 6 alkenyl, C 1 -C 6 alkynyl,ГўВҖВ“NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 , C 1 -C 6 alkoxy, C 1 -C 6 aryloxy, C 1 -C 6 heteroaryloxy, and polyalkylene oxide, each optionally substituted with one or more of halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, ГўВҲВ’OH, C 1 -C 6 alkoxy,ГўВҲВ’NH 2 ,ГўВҲВ’NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 ,ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 - C 6 alkyl) 3 (I),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (Cl), orГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (F); and 

 wherein the compound of formula (II) is: or an acceptable salt thereof, wherein each p is independently an integer selected from 0, 1, and 2; and R 11 , R 12 , R 13 , and R 14 are independently selected from the group consisiting of: halogen, C 1 -C 6 alkyl, C 1 -C 6 alkenyl, C 1 -C 6 alkynyl, C 1 -C 6 alkoxy,ГўВҖВ“ NH(C 1 -C 6 alkyl),ГўВҖВ“N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 2 ,ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (I),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (Cl),ГўВҲВ’N(C 1 -C 6 alkyl) 3 (F),ГўВҲВ’S(O) 2 (OH), andГўВҲВ’S(O) 2 (C 1 -C 6 alkoxy). 10. The composition of claim 9, wherein the photosensitizer is PC1, PC2, PC3.4, PC14, TMAP, or TPPS2. 11. The method claim 2, wherein L 1 and L 2 are independently selected from: wherein L 1 and L 2 are the same. 15.
